content:
Complete the given `Customer` class hierarchy by adding new features following the given instructions. You will be implementing polymorphism through method overriding.

<details>

<summary>**Features to be added to `Customer`, `GuestCustomer`, and `RegisteredCustomer` classes**</summary>

- **Add attributes to `Customer` class**:
  - The `Customer` class should have the following attributes:
    - `id`
    - `name`
    - `address`
    - `email`

- **Add `update_details` method to `Customer` class**:
  - The `update_details` method should print `"Customer details updated"`.

- **Override `update_details` method in `GuestCustomer` class**:
  - The overridden `update_details` method should print `"Guest customer details updated"`.

- **Override `update_details` method in `RegisteredCustomer` class**:
  - The overridden `update_details` method should print `"Registered customer details updated"`.

</details>

<MultiLineNote>

- Do not edit the `default_test` function as this will affect the test cases.
- The output of the testcase **Checking Default Tests** is given by the `default_test` function in the prefilled code. Do not **edit or delete** the `default_test` function.
- This Coding question does not have the usual input/output testcases. The class defined by you will be tested internally whether the methods are present or not. So in testcases results, you will be shown the rough description of the tests that will be verified.

</MultiLineNote>

---

test_cases.input: Checking if correct attribute names are used
test_cases.output: PASS: All attributes are present in Customer class

Customer details updated
Guest customer details updated
Registered customer details updated

test_cases.input: Checking if methods are implemented correctly
test_cases.output: PASS: All update_details methods are present
